message:
docs(readme): sync Data Model enum lists with current fp-types/fp-index (br-2chb)

fd90.88: README Data Model section's ASCII tree boxes were stale:
- DType list: 5 → 8 variants (added Categorical, Timedelta64, Sparse)
- Scalar list: 5 → 6 variants (added Timedelta64(i64))
- IndexLabel description: "Int64 or Utf8" → "Int64/Utf8/Datetime64/Timedelta64"
- Added trailing sentence noting Categorical/Timedelta64/Sparse are extension
  dtypes layered on the core numeric/string buckets

Verified against:
- crates/fp-types/src/lib.rs:68 (DType: 8 variants)
- crates/fp-types/src/lib.rs:120 (Scalar: 6 variants)
- crates/fp-index/src/lib.rs:84 (IndexLabel: 4 variants)

Build passes (cargo build -p frankenpandas).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use interleaved groups so windows prove group-local accumulation and tuple-index row labeling.","notes":"cc-pandas triage 2026-04-22: live pandas oracle for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling mean|sum landed in commit de68f8b; generic fixture dispatch for the same operations wired in today's commit f7a4e59. live_oracle_dataframe_groupby_rolling_{mean,sum}_matches_pandas pass under rch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-22T08:03:09.671999218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:35:01.202297668Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:35:01.199740401Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-29lw","title":"[LOW] hygiene: no .mailmap — swarm identities (cc-pandas / Dicklesworthstone / cod) fragment git shortlog + blame","description":"Swarm author normalization audit.

**Observation.** `ls .mailmap` → No such file.

**Swarm context.** Recent git log shows multiple authorial identities:
- `cc-pandas <jeff141421@gmail.com>` — my commits this session.
- `Dicklesworthstone <jeff141421@gmail.com>` — upstream maintainer identity.
- `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>` — my Co-Authored-By tag.
- Possibly cod, cod-pandas, and other agent identities from prior sessions.

Same underlying email in some cases; different display names. `git log --format="%an"` returns a cluttered list. `git shortlog -sn` — the canonical "who contributed" summary — lists each distinct Author as a separate contributor, even when they're the same identity.

**Impact.**
- Contributor lists on GitHub / GitLab / docs.rs look fragmented.
- `git blame` on a line written by cc-pandas shows "cc-pandas" for one commit, "Dicklesworthstone" for another — the reader can't tell they're the same identity doing slightly different swarm-role work.
- Once br-4clx ships to crates.io, crates.io shows a "Maintained by" summary; fragmented identities make this read oddly.

**Severity.** LOW — hygiene only. No operational impact. Calibrating honestly.

**Fix shape.** `.mailmap` at repo root:
```
# Normalize swarm identities to canonical contributor names.
Dicklesworthstone <jeff141421@gmail.com> cc-pandas <jeff141421@gmail.com>
Dicklesworthstone <jeff141421@gmail.com> cod-pandas <jeff141421@gmail.com>
# Add entries as new agent identities appear.
```

One line per identity mapping. `git log --use-mailmap` (or `git config log.mailmap true`) then renders the canonical name.

Optional companion: AUTHORS.md (br-3d5q covers) lists each swarm agent identity with its role + signing key. .mailmap is the git-level mapping; AUTHORS.md is the human-readable intent.

Blast radius: 1 new tiny file (~10 lines). Zero source changes.

Prerequisites: None. Ship in isolation.

**Related:**
- br-3d5q (commit signing + AUTHORS.md) — companion identity-policy bead.
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — references the mailmap policy.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:00:35.660711166Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:18.242696Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:18.242696Z","close_reason":".mailmap already shipped on main.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hygiene","mailmap","review","swarm"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-2chb","title":"fd90.88: README Data Model dtype/Scalar lists are stale (missing Categorical/Timedelta64/Sparse)","description":"README Data Model section line 249/253 lists DType as {Null, Bool, Int64, Float64, Utf8} and Scalar with 5 variants. Actual fp-types DType has 8 variants: Null, Bool, Int64, Float64, Utf8, Categorical, Timedelta64, Sparse. Actual Scalar has 6 variants: Null, Bool, Int64, Float64, Utf8, Timedelta64. README is missing Categorical, Timedelta64, Sparse on the dtype side and Timedelta64 on the Scalar side. Update both ASCII tree boxes to match actual enum definitions in fp-types/src/lib.rs. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:52:35.719436145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:56:08.270471891Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:56:08.267868742Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","readme","review"]}

@@ -241,20 +241,20 @@ FrankenPandas uses a columnar storage model identical to pandas' internal repres
 
 ```
 DataFrame
-├── index: Index (Vec<IndexLabel>)  ← Row labels (Int64 or Utf8)
+├── index: Index (Vec<IndexLabel>)  ← Row labels (Int64/Utf8/Datetime64/Timedelta64)
 ├── columns: BTreeMap<String, Column>  ← Named columns
 └── column_order: Vec<String>  ← Insertion-order preservation
 
 Column
-├── dtype: DType  ← {Null, Bool, Int64, Float64, Utf8}
+├── dtype: DType  ← {Null, Bool, Int64, Float64, Utf8, Categorical, Timedelta64, Sparse}
 ├── values: Vec<Scalar>  ← Typed values
 └── validity: ValidityMask  ← Bitpacked null bitmap
 
-Scalar = Null(NullKind) | Bool(bool) | Int64(i64) | Float64(f64) | Utf8(String)
+Scalar = Null(NullKind) | Bool(bool) | Int64(i64) | Float64(f64) | Utf8(String) | Timedelta64(i64)
 NullKind = Null | NaN | NaT  ← Three-way null semantics matching pandas
 ```
 
-Every `Scalar` knows its own type, and every `Column` enforces type homogeneity through `DType`. The type promotion hierarchy follows pandas exactly: `Null < Bool < Int64 < Float64` (with Utf8 incompatible with numerics).
+Every `Scalar` knows its own type, and every `Column` enforces type homogeneity through `DType`. The type promotion hierarchy follows pandas exactly: `Null < Bool < Int64 < Float64` (with Utf8 incompatible with numerics). `Categorical`, `Timedelta64`, and `Sparse` are extension dtypes layered on top of the core numeric/string buckets.
 
 ### ValidityMask: Bitpacked Null Tracking
